questions.prompt: Your team is planning weeks of work to build a custom self-service billing UI (card update, plan changes, cancel). What should you do first?

choices.text: Try Stripe's Customer Portal. 30-minute setup; customizable functionality (which features users can self-serve); branded with logo + brand color; webhooks fire on changes so your DB syncs normally. For 90% of subscription management needs, Portal is sufficient and saves weeks of development. Build custom only when you've validated Portal isn't enough.

questions.explanation: Customer Portal is the canonical self-service billing UX. It handles card updates, plan changes, cancellations, invoice history. Configurable, branded, free with Stripe Billing. The premium-brand argument for custom UX rarely outweighs the weeks of dev cost.

questions.prompt: When should you use Stripe Checkout vs. Customer Portal?

choices.text: Checkout for NEW sales (first subscription, one-time purchases). Customer Portal for MANAGING existing subscriptions (update card, change plan, cancel, view invoices). Both are hosted by Stripe; both can coexist in your product. Use the right one for the flow.

questions.explanation: Different purposes: Checkout = new sales; Portal = managing existing subscriptions. Both are Stripe-hosted, both save dev time, both webhook into your system. Use each where it fits.
